### Question B (14 Marks) [KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]

**(a) (7 Marks)** Describe the **safety provisions relating to pressure plants (boilers and air-receivers)** under the Factories Act, 1948. Why is a Certificate of Fitness mandatory?

**(b) (7 Marks)** A steam boiler operates at a working pressure of **12 bar (gauge)**. Calculate the **hydraulic test pressure** required under the Factories Act. If the inner diameter of the shell is **1.4 m**, the wall thickness is **22 mm**, and the longitudinal joint efficiency is **0.85**, verify whether the boiler can withstand a **proof pressure of 18 bar** using the **circumferential (hoop) stress** formula. Take $E = 2.1 \times 10^{5}$ N/mm² and allowable stress $\sigma_{allow} = 130$ N/mm².

#### Model Solution — (a)

The Factories Act, 1948, under **Sections 31 to 35** and the **Indian Boilers Act, 1923**, mandates that:

1. **Every steam boiler** must be **registered** with the **Chief Inspector of Boilers** of the state.
2. The boiler must bear a **Maker's Number** and a **Maker's Name Plate**.
3. A **competent person** (a Boiler Inspector) must inspect the boiler:
   - **Thoroughly once every year** (internal + external).
   - **Hydraulically tested once every two years** (or after major repair).
4. The **Certificate of Fitness** must be displayed **prominently** on the boiler.
5. The certificate is **valid for 12 months** from the date of issue and must be renewed before expiry.
6. **Air-receivers** (compressed-air vessels) have similar provisions under Section 32.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The Certificate of Fitness is mandatory because:
> - It proves the **boiler has been tested at 1.5 × MAWP** without leakage or permanent deformation.
> - It is a **legal defence** for the occupier against prosecution under Section 92.
> - It is a **prerequisite for insurance claims** and is recognized by the Factories Inspectorate as a sign of statutory compliance.

#### Model Solution — (b)

**Step 1 — Hydraulic Test Pressure:**

$$P_{test} = 1.5 \times P_{work} = 1.5 \times 12 = 18\ \text{bar}$$

**Step 2 — Hoop Stress at Proof Pressure (18 bar = 1.8 N/mm²):**

The thin-wall cylinder formula:

$$\sigma_{hoop} = \dfrac{P \cdot d}{2 t}$$

where $P$ is internal pressure, $d$ is internal diameter, $t$ is wall thickness.

$$\sigma_{hoop} = \dfrac{1.8 \times 1400}{2 \times 22} = \dfrac{2520}{44} = 57.27\ \text{N/mm}^2$$

**Step 3 — Longitudinal Stress:**

$$\sigma_{long} = \dfrac{P \cdot d}{4 t} = \dfrac{1.8 \times 1400}{4 \times 22} = \dfrac{2520}{88} = 28.64\ \text{N/mm}^2$$

**Step 4 — Apply Joint Efficiency (reduce allowable stress):**

$$\sigma_{allow,eff} = \eta \times \sigma_{allow} = 0.85 \times 130 = 110.5\ \text{N/mm}^2$$

**Step 5 — Compare and Conclude:**
- $\sigma_{hoop} = 57.27\ \text{N/mm}^2$ — much less than $\sigma_{allow,eff} = 110.5\ \text{N/mm}^2$.
- $\sigma_{long} = 28.64\ \text{N/mm}^2$ — also much less than $\sigma_{allow,eff}$.

> [!NOTE]
> **Conclusion:** The boiler can safely withstand the proof pressure of 18 bar. The **hydraulic test pressure of 18 bar (1.5 × 12 bar)** is exactly satisfied, and both hoop and longitudinal stresses are well within the allowable limit. The boiler **passes** the statutory hydraulic test.

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Common Pitfalls:**
> 1. **Forgetting the 1.5× multiplier** for hydraulic test pressure — this costs 1 mark every time.
> 2. **Not using joint efficiency** when the problem statement gives it — students mistakenly use σ_allow directly.
> 3. **Confusing circumferential and longitudinal stresses** — remember: hoop is twice the longitudinal for the same P, d, t.
> 4. **Wrong unit conversion** — 1 bar = 0.1 N/mm². Mixing bar and N/mm² is the #1 error.
> 5. **In noise-dosage problems**, students often invert $T_i$ (write $T_i / C_i$ instead of $C_i / T_i$).

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Rapid-Revision Checklist for "Safety Provisions in Factories Act"**

- [ ] **Factory definition (S. 2(m)):** 10 workers with power / 20 workers without power.
- [ ] **Crowding (S. 16):** ≥ 14.2 m³ per worker.
- [ ] **Temperature (S. 14):** ≥ 15.5 °C, ≤ outdoor wet-bulb.
- [ ] **Lighting (S. 17):** General ≥ 100 lux, fine ≥ 200 lux.
- [ ] **Fencing (S. 19):** All dangerous parts; minimum height 2.1 m.
- [ ] **Work on moving machinery (S. 20):** Only trained male adults ≥ 18 years, with written authorization.
- [ ] **Striking gear / cut-off (S. 21):** Every prime mover + flywheel must have both.
- [ ] **Confined-space entry (S. 28):** Gas-test → 5 % LEL rule; rescue equipment at entry.
- [ ] **Boilers (S. 31–35):** Register with Chief Inspector; annual inspection; 1.5 × MAWP hydraulic test; Certificate of Fitness valid 12 months.
- [ ] **Explosive atmosphere (S. 36):** Flame-proof fittings, anti-static measures, no naked flames.
- [ ] **PPE (S. 38):** Free of cost to worker; conforming to BIS.
- [ ] **Safety Officer (S. 40-B):** Mandatory for factories with ≥ 1000 workers or carrying out hazardous processes.
- [ ] **Noise dose formula (Rule 36-A):** $D = \sum C_i / T_i \le 1$; 90 dB(A) reference; 5 dB exchange rate.
- [ ] **Hoop stress (cylindrical vessel):** $\sigma = Pd / 2t$.
- [ ] **Longitudinal stress (cylindrical vessel):** $\sigma = Pd / 4t$.
- [ ] **Joint efficiency adjustment:** $\sigma_{allow,eff} = \eta \times \sigma_{allow}$.

> [!NOTE]
> **Last-minute mnemonics:**
> - "**C**lean **V**entilate **L**ight **D**rink **C**rowd **F**ire" → Sections 11, 13, 17, 15, 16, 17.
> - "**FENCE** before **START**" → Fencing (S.19) + Striking gear (S.21) before machine start.
> - "**1.5** is the magic number" — hydraulic test = 1.5 × MAWP.
